This action plan is meant to supplement the Google Ads Manual (10xdownload.com/ga1) to
make sure you’re crystal clear on the actions to take to get your desired results. When in
doubt, refer to the manual!
While this is meant to empower you to get ads up and running, keep in mind that they
can ultimately be run by a team member or an agency. Good luck!
SET UP ALL NEEDED ACCOUNTS	STEP
1
These are all the things you need to run ads...
Google Account (if you don’t already have one)
• Google Ad Manager Tour Video (10xresource.com/ga1)
• Prep and Setup Checklist (10xdownload.com/ga5)
• Google Signup Instructions (10xresource.com/ga4)

SET UP WEBSITE CONVERSION TRACKING	STEP
3
Your Google Tag is what will help you track results and send important
information back to Google based on user activity (visits, opt-ins, purchases, etc).
These activities are referred to as Conversions.
Conversion data helps Google best optimize your ads and give you the
opportunity to create highly targeted audiences. You only need one Google Tag
and it should be installed on every website and funnel you own.
Having funnels that convert is essential for ad success. See the 10X Sales Funnel
System to learn how to maximize sales on every click (10xresource.com/ga10).
• Ad Strategy Flowchart (10xdownload.com/ga3)
Recommended Resources:
• Google Conversion Overview (10xresource.com/ga11)
• Google Conversion Installation (10xresource.com/ga12)
Recommended Resources:
CREATE A BASIC STRATEGY	STEP
2
Before running ads, you will need to have a clear idea of...
What website or landing pages will you send traffic to?
How much are you willing to invest in learning ads?
What is your break even point for acquiring customers?
Who is going to be checking these ads on a daily basis?
Why are you running ads? (motivation for work and cost)

-- 2 of 5 --

LAUNCH YOUR FIRST AD CAMPAIGN	STEP
4
You will create many ads, so keep it simple at first to get up and running.
MONITOR, TEST, TWEAK AND SCALE	STEP
5
You should check on your ads daily and make adjustments as needed. There are
many factors involved, but ultimately, you will be trying new audiences (ad groups)
and adjusting budgets based on performance.
Note that (fundamentally) Google will also be working to optimize your ads. The
algorithm’s job is to help you succeed by seeking out the right people to display
your ads to. Be sure to give this process adequate time.
• Search Ad Setup Video (10xresource.com/ga2)
• Video Ad Setup Video (10xresource.com/ga3)
• Campaign Structure Flowchart (10xdownload.com/ga4)
• Google Ad Creation Checklist (10xdownload.com/ga6)
• Google Ad Swipe Files (10xresource.com/fba7)
Recommended Resources:
If you have a video to run as an ad, get it on YouTube
If not, you’ll likely want to start by running a search ad
As you will see in the provided swipe files, ads don’t need to be fancy to make
money. For example, you can use your phone to record video ads.
Follow Google’s step-by-step setup, and whenever in doubt, refer to the
checklists and manual!

-- 3 of 5 --

MAKE YOUR FIRST SALE AND CELEBRATE!	STEP
6
Keep in mind that Google Ad success may not happen overnight. Results often
come in breakthroughs, and when it does, it can be highly profitable!
It is also important that you are continually creating new ads while testing
existing ones. This will help fight ad fatigue, and more importantly, help you find
the winning message for your audience.
NOTE: More details are provided in the manual as well as on the checklist
overview video.
We recommend testing every type of Google Ad that is relevant to your business…
• Google Ad Optimization Checklist (10xdownload.com/ga7)
Recommended Resource:
Search (text based ads on Google search results)
Display (banner ads appearing on other websites)
Video (video based ads primarily on YouTube)
Local (specific to physical geo targeted locations)
Shopping (product listings on various channels)
App (promote an app on various channels)
Discover (various properties such as gmail)
Performance Max
GO TO THE NEXT LEVEL!
